what_it_does: This file is a captured idea/noticeboard item that frames a walk-through artifact for a user (Tim) to shape five open design questions about an L5 'propose-verb' feature where the RHM ('V') can propose refinements to Tim's decision cards, pending his explicit accept or reject.
observations: It is a walk-through artifact intended for ChatGPT to guide Tim and collect his feedback.
It describes a feature where the RHM proposes refinements to a decision card, the proposal surfaces as pending with rationale, and Tim accepts or rejects each one.
It states that refinements are never auto-applied; the already-decided posture is 'let it propose refinements — you accept each'.
It notes a gap: pending proposals existed but were not being surfaced, making them invisible.
It lists five open design questions for Tim to shape: proactivity, scope, prominence, accept/reject UX, and autonomy.
It reports status: the reversible engine fork is proceeding, while the projection/DNA user-facing render is holding until Tim's answers arrive.
It has YAML front matter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, created, updated, and a single history transition.
No code symbols are present in the file.
standouts: The explicit governance rule: propose-then-Tim-accepts, never auto-applied.
The five numbered open design questions meant for user shaping before UI build.
The deliberate split between engine work (proceeding) and user-facing projection/DNA (holding) based on feedback.
conventions: Uses YAML front matter with metadata and a history array.
Uses Markdown headings, bold emphasis, and numbered lists for design questions.
Refers to project-specific concepts such as L5, RHM, FACE-2, card-refine-posture, projection/DNA, and fork without defining them here.
No code symbols, imports, or technical implementation details are included.
concerns: Heavy reliance on project-specific jargon that is not defined within this file.
The file calls itself a walk-through artifact but does not contain the actual conversational script or user-facing questions; it is a design brief.
History contains only the initial capture transition and no later updates.
notes: A design-brief noticeboard item asking Tim to shape an RHM proposal-refinement feature via five UX/autonomy questions before the user-facing render is built.
questions: What do 'L5', 'RHM', 'FACE-2', and 'projection/DNA' specifically denote in this project's taxonomy?
What is the technical shape of the '/api/decision/proposals' read and the 'refine role + propose verb'?
How are decision cards rendered and where do pending proposals currently appear?
What is the exact relationship between the reversible engine fork and the projection/DNA hold?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to be a walk-through artifact for ChatGPT to take Tim through design questions and collect feedback; the file itself is a design brief framing those questions and feature context, not the interactive walk-through script, so it partially matches.
apparent_themes: Explicit accept/reject control rather than automatic application.
Separation of reversible engine development from user-facing projection.
Active RHM presence per user decision.
intention_signals: The file is trying to establish a structured feedback loop where the user shapes an RHM-driven refinement feature before the UI is committed, ensuring the RHM's proactive suggestions remain visible, controllable, and scoped to user preference.
novelty: It treats an AI assistant's refinement suggestions as first-class pending proposals on decision cards with explicit accept/reject governance, and it deliberately holds the user-facing render until the user shapes five dimensions of the interaction.
contribution: It captures a feature concept and a set of open design dimensions (proactivity, scope, prominence, accept/reject UX, autonomy) that a fused system would keep as the shaping brief for the RHM proposal-refinement capability.
summary_for_embedding: A captured idea noticeboard item describing an L5 'propose-verb' feature where the RHM can propose refinements to a user's decision cards, pending explicit accept/reject, and listing five open design questions for user shaping before building the user-facing render.
intention_for_embedding: To frame a walk-through and feedback loop for Tim to shape how RHM refinement proposals are generated, surfaced, and accepted or rejected on decision cards.
